title: Reversible Merge Sort on Doubly Linked List

statement.markdown: Given two sorted doubly linked lists head1 and head2, merge them into one sorted doubly linked list in reverse order. Note that the nodes in both lists are 0-indexed.
statement.inputFormat: Input a node as an integer, where the first line is the number of nodes in the first list and the second line is the list, followed by the number of nodes in the second list and the second list.
statement.outputFormat: The output is a single line showing the merged and sorted doubly linked list in reverse order.
statement.notes: This function should reverse the lists and then merge them while maintaining the sorted order.

examples.explanation: Merge the two sorted doubly linked lists in reverse order.

hints.text: Reverse the lists first before merging them.
Use two pointers to merge the lists.

editorial.title: Merging Two Doubly Linked Lists
editorial.intuition: Reverse the input linked lists, then use two pointers to merge them in the reverse order.
editorial.bruteForce: Use a temporary list to store the merged result, then reverse the list.
editorial.betterSolution: Merge the lists directly while maintaining the sorted order.
editorial.optimalSolution: Reverse the lists first, then use two pointers to merge them in the reverse order.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The merged list is sorted in reverse order.
editorial.dryRun: Example dry run of the correct solution.
commonMistakes: Mistaking the order of reversal and merging.

functionSignature.java: public void solve(DoublyListNode head1, DoublyListNode head2);
